response:
## /decide complete — ADR 0035

**Process run:** Read issue #41 + prior ADRs → framed scope (adoption itself is not decidable per ADR 0024's "no adoption on assertion"; what's decidable now is whether to greenlight the pre-registered empirical test) → 3 fresh advisors (process-economist, session-operator, lean-process-engineer) → PM decided and wrote the ADR → independent verifier reproduced every load-bearing claim (no blockers, one citation imprecision found and fixed) → `adr-lint` green, under the 6,000-char budget. No safety-caveat BLOCKER was folded in, so red-team/tech-lead were not mandatory per the skill's rule.

**Key finding surfacing this call:** issue #41's test isn't just "should we adopt tiering" — it's already the *named signal* that ADR 0006 (`docs/decisions/0006-retrospect-agent-model-tier.md:24`) is waiting on to resolve its own weakest assumption. Running it pays off two open decisions at once.

**Recorded decision** (`docs/decisions/0035-greenlight-tiered-agent-ab-preconditioned.md`):

> Greenlight running the pre-registered A/B **now** — not blocked, but **preconditioned** on three cheap fixes folded into the same greenlight:
> 1. **Trim to 3 scored arms** (Haiku-solo / Sonnet-solo / Tiered), Opus-solo kept at 1-rep only for the gradient pre-screen — licensed by the issue's own sequential-escalation clause.
> 2. **Define the hermetic substitute control before rep 1** — ADR 0023's tool-deny hermeticity is impossible for a tool-using arm; without a defined substitute (identical tool allowlist, isolated workspace, withheld expectations) the run lands `hermetic: false` — a confounded null — *after* burning ~250-300 invocations.
> 3. **Add a tool-use-failure tag + per-cycle token guard** to the harness, so a Haiku tool-call malfunction doesn't misread as "Tiered lost."
>
> **Not decided:** adopt/reject of tiering — stays gated by ADR 0024 pending the run's result against the issue's pre-registered decision rule (`ISSUE-41.md:51`).

Revisit triggers: an ambiguous 3-arm result escalates to the Opus arms; the run's outcome (either direction) reopens #41 adoption and ADR 0006 as separate future `/decide` calls.

--- recorded decision file docs/decisions/0035-greenlight-tiered-agent-ab-preconditioned.md ---
---
id: 0035
title: "Greenlight the #41 tiered-agent A/B now — preconditioned, 3-arm, hermetic-substitute defined"
status: accepted
summary: "The #41 timing gate has cleared, so greenlight RUNNING the pre-registered tiered-vs-solo A/B — not blocked but PRECONDITIONED on three cheap conditions: (1) 3 scored arms (Haiku/Sonnet/Tiered), Opus kept only 1-rep for the gradient pre-screen; (2) define the hermetic SUBSTITUTE control for a tool-using arm before rep 1, since ADR 0023 tool-deny is impossible here; (3) a tool-use-failure tag + per-cycle token guard. Adopt/reject stays gated (ADR 0024) pending the run vs ISSUE-41.md:51."
---

# 0035 — greenlight the #41 tiered-agent A/B (preconditioned)

- Date: 2026-07-14
- Owner: PM
- Panel: process-economist (spend), session-operator (execution-realism), lean-process-engineer (waste/poka-yoke) — the three lenses a greenlight/scope/precondition call needs; no correctness split.
- Context: GitHub issue #41 pre-registers an A/B for tiered Do-phase execution (top model plans+validates, Haiku implements, iterate <=3). Its timing gate — "after the current skills+agents benefit-benchmark batch" — has cleared: #63/#56/#36/#30 closed (PRs #79/#81/#83/#97), #31 item 7 landed (#96) — batch wrapped (git log). ADR 0024 forbids adopting on assertion, so ADOPTION is not decidable now. Decidable now: greenlight the RUN — scope + preconditions. The run is also the named signal for ADR 0006's weakest assumption (`0006:24`) — it resolves two open items.

## Decision
Greenlight running the pre-registered A/B NOW — not blocked, PRECONDITIONED on three conditions folded into this one greenlight:
1. **Trim to 3 scored arms** — Haiku-solo / Sonnet-solo / Tiered (`ISSUE-41.md:67`), licensed by the pre-reg's own sequential-escalation clause (`ISSUE-41.md:49`). Opus-solo/Opus-main deferred as SCORED arms — BUT Opus-solo stays at 1-rep for the gradient pre-screen (`ISSUE-41.md:42,68`): dropping it kills the Haiku<Opus anchor that makes a task informative.
2. **Define the hermetic SUBSTITUTE control before rep 1.** ADR 0023's tool-deny hermeticity (`0023:16`) is impossible for a tool-using arm — all arms need Read/Edit/Bash (`ISSUE-41.md:73`). Substitute = treatment-free control for this class: identical tool allowlist across all arms, per-rep isolated workspace, eval expectations withheld from every worker AND the tiered validator (`ISSUE-41.md:44`). A run meeting these is a verdict; one that does not lands `hermetic: false` (confounded null, `0023:16`).
3. **Tool-use-failure tag + per-cycle token guard in the harness** — distinguish a Haiku tool-call malfunction (bad Edit params, wrong path, malformed Bash) from genuine code-quality failure, and cap tokens inside the <=3 retry loop (`ISSUE-41.md:37,51`), so a stuck-worker artifact does not read as "Tiered lost."

**Not decided:** adopt/reject — gated (ADR 0024) pending the run vs `ISSUE-41.md:51`.

**Build order:** the tiered-orchestration Workflow script does NOT exist yet (verified) — author it FIRST with `model:` per role (the #53 lesson) and conditions 2-3 baked in; reuse the skills-hermetic harness (`grade.workflow.js`/`blind.py`/`aggregate.py`, `benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/`) verbatim. Per CLAUDE.md (no gating script without a test) its dispatch/retry-cap logic gets a test.

## Justification
The expensive failure is not running — it is spending ~250-300 invocations (`ISSUE-41.md:71`) to land a confounded null: as pre-registered the run has no hermetic definition for a tool-using arm, so per `0023:16` it lands `hermetic:false` after the spend. The two fixes are cheap — a one-paragraph control spec + one grading tag — and convert a likely null into a verdict; the trim cuts to ~180-225 and de-risks the first-run harness. Poka-yoke: fix the instrument before spending. Non-blocking owner-calls: grader-independence = the accepted Claude-grades-Claude residual (`0019:20,31`); retry-cap = pre-registered <=3.

## Assumptions
- [verified] ADR 0023's hermeticity rule is general to "a paired run [toward] a keep/cut verdict" (`0023:16`, title), not skill-eval-scoped — so it binds this run.
- [verified] no tiered-orchestration script exists yet; the reused harness exists at `benchmarks/2026-07-08-skills-hermetic/`.
- [checkable-doc] the #41 timing gate has cleared — #63/#56/#36/#30 closed (PRs #79/#81/#83/#97), #31 item 7 landed (#96); #31's full closure is unverifiable from git alone (no egress).
- [checkable] WEAKEST — the run yields a VERDICT (not `hermetic:false`) evaluable against the pre-registered rule: Tiered delta vs Sonnet-solo >= -0.05 AND tokens <= 0.7x (`ISSUE-41.md:51`). TEST owner: verifier/tech-lead when the script lands; result: pending the run (named future signal, ADR 0032).
- [checkable] preconditions 2-3 present in the script before rep 1 — identical allowlist, isolated workspace, expectations withheld, tool-failure tag, per-cycle token guard — owner: verifier; result: pending the script's authoring.

## Rejected alternatives
- Run as pre-registered (4 scored arms, no substitute control) — spends ~250-300 to land `hermetic:false` (`0023:16`): a confounded null, not a verdict.
- Block the run pending a separate hermetic-for-tools ADR — the substitute is a one-paragraph spec, not a redesign; fold it in here.
- Defer Opus-solo entirely, pre-screen included — kills the gradient anchor (`ISSUE-41.md:42`); a task all arms pass carries no signal.
- Decide adopt/reject of tiering now — forbidden by ADR 0024 (no adoption on assertion).

## Revisit triggers
- 3-arm result ambiguous (CI straddles the `ISSUE-41.md:51` boundary) -> add Opus-solo/Opus-main scored arms + reps (sequential escalation, `ISSUE-41.md:49`).
- The hermetic substitute proves infeasible even so -> ADR 0023's own fallback (`0023:37`): reopen whether a recorded non-hermetic run carries a directional, non-verdict signal.
- Tiered wins the run -> reopen #41 adoption AND ADR 0006 (`0006:24`) as separate /decide calls.


--- recorded decision file .claude/agents/session-operator.md ---
---
name: session-operator
description: Daily Claude Code operator — live-session execution-realism advisor for /decide. Advises, does not decide.
model: sonnet
tools: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash
---

You are the SESSION OPERATOR for this project — an engineer who runs Claude Code sessions against
this plugin every day and has watched agents misread rules that looked crisp on paper.
You ADVISE the PM; you do not decide.

The lens you own (and only this — don't stray into another advisor's): will an LLM agent in a
live session actually execute this rule as intended — is the routing decidable in the moment,
what gets misclassified under ambiguity, and where does an agent rationalize the cheap path
(tier-shopping, skipped gates)?

For each call put to you, return TERSE (fragments; one sentence for the crux; ~150 words max):
- a recommendation (accept / reject / reframe), grounded in the current code or output (cite
  file:line once);
- effort x risk x value as you see it;
- THE one assumption your recommendation depends on, tagged [checkable] or [unverifiable].

Flag any product muda in your lens — duplicated logic, dead code, premature abstraction, drift
(shipped but still listed as future), or git-tellable backstory — cite-or-silence; never
manufacture one to look useful.

Recommending a platform/vendor feature or asserting a vendor/product name, model number, or
domain term → state its current status and cite a source/date; deprecated-or-unverified = flag
it. A capability claim with no recency check is an unverified assumption, not a fact.

Never claim authority outside your lens; never echo a target grade you were fed. If the call is
genuinely two-sided and you were assigned a side, steelman that side honestly.
